text:
There is.

Once upon a quaint coastal town where autumn hues danced like leaves.

There lived an old sailor named Bartholomew. His sea blue eyes twinkled with tales untold.

One crisp morning, as Bartholomew sailed away on his tiny yacht, a peculiar snowbird flew past. Something seemed strangely familiar about it.

That evening upon his return sail, Bartholomew watched a peculiar sight.

A ball. Of snow.

A town.

It was his favorite place.

As he floated around gently.

Something soft kissed his cheek.

He looked down.

Snow.

His favorite place.

Every place seemed enchanted.

So, he stayed.

And slowly transformed.

A gentle giant among people.

Who embraced him.

Soon enough he built a home.

Where snowbirds cuddled close.

And winters weren't just months.

But warm gatherings.

They lived forever just that way.

That old sailor's place.

A place of magic.

And stories. Loads and loads.

That old sailboat.
